title: Strengthening Actions Taken To Adjust Imports of Aluminum, Steel, and Copper Into the United States
summary: This proclamation announces adjustments to import tariffs on aluminum, steel, and copper to safeguard U.S. national security. It reaffirms the findings that these metals are crucial for national security and that their imports, under certain conditions, threaten this security. The proclamation modifies existing tariff regimes by applying duties to the full customs value of metal articles and their derivatives, regardless of metal content. Specific ad valorem duty rates are set, with variations for products from the United Kingdom and those made entirely from U.S.-origin metals. The proclamation also terminates previous inclusion processes for derivative articles, authorizing the Secretary of Commerce and the U.S. Trade Representative to include additional derivative articles on a rolling basis if they threaten national security. Key findings indicate that existing tariffs have strengthened domestic aluminum and steel industries, increasing capacity utilization. The modifications aim to further strengthen domestic manufacturing, reduce reliance on foreign imports, encourage domestic production capacity and R&D, and train workers for strategic production. The proclamation also details specific duty rates, exceptions, and procedures for implementation and monitoring, including reporting requirements and potential revocations of benefits for certain trading partners.
